rulings.Case Title: LEGALINK INC (FKA COMBS AND GREENLEY, INC) VS. JAY JACKMAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Monetary Sanctions For Failure To Answer Post-Judgment Discovery
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, December 30, 2011, Line 1. PLAINTIFF COMBS AND GREENLEY, INC'S Notice Of Monetary Sanctions For Failure To Answer Post-Judgment Discovery. The motion is off calendar because the moving party failed to provide a courtesy copy of the moving papers in compliance with San Francisco Local Rule 2.6B. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: ALEXA RUDIN et al VS. HILL & COMPANY REAL ESTATE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Leave To Amend And File Second Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, December 30, 2011, Line 1. PLAINTIFF ALEXA RUDIN, ETHAN RUDIN Motion For Leave To Amend And File Second Amended Complaint. Granted, with ten days leave to amend, as to the amendment of allegations against the Estate of Malcolm Post and the prayer for punitive damages as against Defendant Fetter. Denied as to the allegations regarding Plaintiffs'claims regarding the exaggeration of square footage. Plaintiff has not shown good cause for this amendment in light of Plaintiff's admitted knowledge of the allegation since "sometime in the third quarter of 2009,"and the complaint has been amended since that time. (See First Amended Complaint, filed June 30, 2010.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, December 30, 2011, Line 2. PLAINTIFFs ALEXA RUDIN AND ETHAN RUDIN'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order And Request For Monetary Sanctions. Parties are to meet and confer in good faith on the amount of time necessary for a reasonable conclusion of the Rudin deposition, and to address this topic at the hearing tomorrow. = (302/MJM)
